effect.non-transmissible: <0>Non transmissible</0>: this disaster can never be exchanged by discarding a die or a result token.

effect.war-defense: <0>War</0>: when attacked, add +{bonus} <1/> to your total.

effect.war-defense.x: <0>War</0>: when attacked, add +{bonus} <1/> to your total for each <2/>.
effect.war-attack: <0>War</0>: when attacking, add +{bonus} <1/> to your total.
effect.war-attack.if: <0>War</0>: when attacking, add +{bonus} <1/> to your total if <2/>.
effect.earn-gold: Earn {gold} <0/> when you acquire this card.
effect.general: During <0>war</0>, each <1>GENERAL</1> (tilted or not) in your Civilization Zone allows you to reroll all or part of the dice once.
effect.trade-calamity: During your turn, if <0>{card}</0> is in your Event Area, trade it for any card in another player's Event Area.
effect.artillery: <0>War</0>: destroy <1>Artillery</1> to automatically win a 3-point victory (attack or defense).
effect.ransom: Upon acquisition, each opponent must give you 5 <0/> or destroy an active card in their Civilization Area.
effect.turn-coat: <0/> If you lose a <1>war</1>, {card} joins your opponent's civilization area.
effect.seize: If <0>{card1}</0> is in play (in a Civilization Area and not in <1>decay</1>) when you acquire {card2}, move it to your Civilization Area.
effect.counterattack: If a player attacks you and wins the battle, you counter-attack: Gain a free <0>War</0> action usable immediately against your attacker.
effect.robin-hood: Upon acquisition, the richest player among the other players gives you all his or her gold <0/> (you choose the player in the event of a tie). Then give half that gold (rounded down) to the player of your choice.
effect.acquire-trade: If <0>{card1}</0> is in play (in a Civilization Area and not in <1>decay</1>) when you acquire {card2}, you can exchange it for a non-calamity card from your Event Area.
effect.swap: On acquisition, swap {card} with any card from any player's Civilization Area.
effect.poison: On acquisition, choose a player who must destroy a <0/> card in his Civilization Area.
effect.cost-per-bonus: This card costs as much <0/> as the number of active bonuses in your Civilization Area.
effect.al-khawarizmi: At the end of the game, the victory point value of <0>Al Khawarizmi</0> is equal to your <1/>/3, maximum 4.
condition.mangonel: your opponent has a <0/>, <1>{card1}</1> or <2>{card2}</2> card
calamity.failure: On your turn as the active player, if this card is in your Events area after you have used your dice, you will suffer the following effect:
calamity.lose-card: <0>Discard an active card</0> from your Civilization area. If a card is in decay below it, it becomes active again.
calamity.lose-bonus: You must discard <0>a bonus card</0> in priority.
calamity.lose-pop-bonus: You must discard a card with <0>a population bonus</0> in priority.
calamity.lose-pop-or-bonus: You must first discard a card with <0>a population bonus</0>. If you don't have one, you must first discard <0>another bonus card</0>.
calamity.civil-war: You must first discard: a <1/>Wonder, otherwise a <2/>Figure, otherwise <0>a card with a bonus</0>.
calamity.replace-events: Then discard all the cards in your Event area and replace them.
calamity.lose-most-vp: You must first discard the card that earns you the most <0/>victory points.
calamity.piracy:
Choose between paying 10 <0/> (if possible) or <1>discarding an active card</1>.
Then <2>Piracy</2> is discarded, and the discard is shuffled into the deck.
calamity.transmit: Then choose a player and give him <0>{card}</0>. This player will exceptionally have an extra card in the Event Area.
calamity.cholera: If you don't have <0>Avicenna</0>, you must first discard a <1/>, otherwise a card with a bonus.
calamity.black-death: If you don't have <0>Avicenna</0>, you must first discard a card with a bonus.
calamity.tithe: Distribute 12 <0/> equally among your opponents. If you can't, destroy a card in your Civilization Area.
card.event-area: This card is in the <0>Events area</0> of {player}.
card.event-area.you: This card is in your <0>Events area</0>.
card.civ-area: This card is in the <0>Civilisation area</0> of {player}.
card.civ-area.you: This card is in your <0>Civilisation area</0>.
card.decayed: This card is <0>in decay</0> in the <1>Civilisation area</1> of {player}.
card.decayed.you: This card is <0>in decay</0> in your <1>Civilisation area</1>.
card.discard-pile: This card is in <0>the discard pile</0>.
event-area.rules:
In this area are the opportunities and dangers your civilization faces.
On your turn, you can move a card from your Events area into your <0>Civilization area</0> by paying its cost.
You can also discard a die or result token to swap a card in your Events area with another player's.
At the end of your turn, draw cards until you once again have 3 cards in your Events area.
board.title: Achievements board
achievements.rules:
On your turn, your <0>civilization token</0> can move forward on the Accomplishments board along the paths marked out to take an Accomplishment token (or not), or move back one space. Vertical movement is allowed.
Each Accomplishment token has a different prerequisite for being taken, and yields a number of victory points, indicated on the back, between 1 and 7.
When you take an Accomplishment token, you must put a card <1>in decadence</1>.
Accomplishment tokens can be stolen from other players by <2>waging war</2>.
civ-area.rules:
When a player acquires a card, he places it in their Civilization area.
As long as a card is in the player's Civilization area, he benefits from its symbol and victory points.
He also benefits from any bonuses the card may have, unless it is placed <0>in decay</0>.
There is no limit to the number of cards that can be placed in the Civilization area.
decay.title: Decay
decay.rules:
When a player acquires an Accomplishment token and has several active cards in his Civilization area, he must place one of his active cards in decadence by sliding it under another active card.
A decadent card becomes inactive: it loses its name and any bonuses, but still yields its symbol and victory points.
<0>Please note: at the end of the age, each population bonus or resource of your decadent cards causes you to lose 2 victory points.</0>
deck.count: There are {cards, plural, one{# card} other{# cards}} left in the deck.
deck.rules:
A game of Along History is played in 1, 2 or 3 ages.
An age ends when a player takes the Accomplishment token of value 7, or more rarely if the deck is empty.
achievement.value: This token yields {value, plural, one{# victory point} other{# victory points}}.
achievement.steal: This token belongs to {player}, but can be stolen by declaring <0>war</0> on them.
achievement.steal.you: This token belongs to you, but another player can steal it by declaring <0>war</0> on you.
achievement.cards: <0>You must have {cards, plural, one{# card} other{# cards}}</0> in your Civilization area (<1>in decay</1> or not) to take this token.
achievement.card-type: <0>You must have {cards, plural, one{# card} other{# cards}} of type {type}</0> in your Civilization area (<1>in decay</1> or not) to take this token.
achievement.diff-types: <0>You must have at least {types} different types of cards</0> in your Civilization area (<1>in decay</1> or not) to take this token.
achievement.victory-points:
<0>Cards in your Civilization area</0> (<1>in decay</1> or not) must bring you at least <0>{number} victory points</0> to take this token.
<0>Please note, victory points from tokens do not count.</0>
achievement.bonus: The <1>active</1> cards in your Civilization zone must bring you at least <0>{bonus} bonus (population or resources)</0> to take this token.
achievement.bonus.pop: The <1>active</1> cards in your Civilization zone must bring you at least <0>{bonus} population bonus</0> to take this token.
achievement.gold: <0>You must have at least {gold} gold</0> to take this token.
universal-resource.rules:
This token can be used to produce <0/>, <1/>, <2/> or <3/> when acquiring a card.
It can also be discarded to <5>declare war</5> on another player.
There are 2 ways to receive a Universal Resource token:
- by <4>building a wonder</4>;
- at the end of a turn in which you have <4>acquired no new cards</4>.
You can never have more than 2 Universal Resource tokens.
discard-tile.rules:
The discard tile is placed in front of the active player.
This player discards the dice there as they are used.
result-token.flipped: This token is returned, having already been used.
result-token.rules:
At the end of the active player's turn, the next player gets the tokens corresponding to the faces of the dice played by the active player (with the exception of special red dice).
Each other player takes a turn with these tokens.
Result tokens can be used to acquire a card, or to swap a card from its event area with another player's card.
dice.type.1.rules:
The three green dice (Population) can earn <0>between 1 and 3 Population points</0>.
<1/>x3
<2/>x2
<3/>x1
dice.type.2.rules:
The blue die earns <0>a resource</0>:
<1/>x2: Ingenuity
<2/>x2: Strength
<3/>x2: Culture
dice.type.3.rules: The two special red dice are exclusive to the active player.
die.reroll.rule: <0/> Reroll (2 sides): discarding this die allows you to reroll all or some of the dice whose results are not to your liking.
die.multiplier.rule: <0/> Multiplier (1 side): you can double the value of the result of another of your Population, Resource, Currency dice, the War or x2 result of the other red die. Example: x2 x2 associated with 4 Gold = 16 Gold
die.war.rule: <0/> War (1 side): you can <1>declare War</1> on another player at the end of your turn.
die.golden-age.rule: <0/> "Golden Age" (2 sides): straightens all tilted cards in your civilization zone. This means you can benefit from your bonuses several times during your turn.
dice.type.4.rules:
The black die (Money) earns <0>the amount shown in gold coins</0>.
<1/>x3
<2/>x1
<3/>x1
<4/>x1
coin.rules:
Silver coins are worth 1 and gold coins are worth 5.

Gold coins are kept in your play area until they are spent or lost. Gold is retained when moving from one Age to another.

Some cards display an alternative Gold cost, in addition to the other costs. These cards can either be acquired normally with Population and Resources, or be purchased with Gold the player's choice. You can choose to spend gold at any time during your turn to buy a card from your Event Area that has a Gold cost and transfer it to your Civilization Area.
war.rules.title: Wars
war.rules.1:
After your actions, you have the option of declaring one or more wars on your opponents.
When you declare war, <0>it means you try to steal an Accomplishment token</0> from the other player.
The defender cannot steal an Accomplishment token from the attacker.
war.rules.2:
In Along History, <0>there are two ways to declare war</0> on another player: using a "Universal Resources" token, or the <2/> (War) symbol on the red die. Both effects are similar.
A die <3/> can multiply a die <4/>. You can declare war twice on the same player.
war.rules.3:
The war takes place in two stages. First, the attacker can <0>tilt population bonuses</0> in his civilization area to strengthen his army. Then <0>he rolls the 3 population dice</0>. The total obtained represents the strength of his army.
Then the defender does the same.
If the attacker defeats the defender, he steals <0>a token whose maximum value is equal to the difference in strength between the two armies</0>.
In the event of defeat, or if there are no eligible tokens, nothing happens.
attacked.detail: <0>{player}</0> attacks you with a <0>force of {strength}</0>. It's time to defend yourself!
wonders.bonus: <0/>: upon acquisition, you earn a <1>Universal Resource token</1>.
rules.winner:
The winner of the game is the player who has accumulated the most victory points during the Age(s) of the game played.
In the event of a tie, the player with the highest-value Achievement token wins.
If the tie persists, the second-highest-value token is used to break the tie, and so on. In the event of a tie, the players share the win.
player.dialog.score.decay: {score} points for the bonus in decay
player.dialog.score.decay.help: Each bonus (losange) on a card under decay is worth -2 points
player.dialog.card-type: {player} has {cards, plural, one{# card} other{# cards}} of type {type}
tuto.options: In Along History, you take on 1 to 4 opponents in a game lasting 1, 2 or 3 ages: <0>Prehistory</0>, <0>Antiquity</0> and <0>The Middle Ages</0>.
tuto.setup: In this tutorial, you'll take on NostroBoss and Cécile during 1 single age, <0>Prehistory</0>.
tuto.goal: Your goal is to have the most <0>victory points</0> at the end of the game.
tuto.event-area: This is your <0>Event Area</0>. These three cards are your opportunities and dangers.
tuto.round1: This is the first round, and you're the active player: you rolled the dice for this round.
tuto.pop-dice: These three green dice and this blue die represent <0>the population and resource</0> you can use to acquire cards this turn.
tuto.card-cost:
The cost of the cards is shown <0>at the top left</0> of them.
For example, you could use 4 <2/> and <3/> to acquire the <1>Tiger</1>.
tuto.reroll: Instead, let's try to reroll the blue die to get <2/> in order to acquire the <1>Forest</1>: <0>discard the second red die to reroll the blue die</0>.
tuto.reroll-success: You've got the <2/> you needed to acquire the <1>Forest</1>!
tuto.trade1:
Before we acquire the forest, let's get rid of that dangerous <1>Tiger</1>, which is costing us 1 population point...
Discard the <2/> die to exchange the <1>Tiger</1> with NostroBoss's <1>Hunting</1> card (who may not appreciate the gift...).
tuto.forest: Now move the <1>Forest</1> into your <0>Civilization Zone</0> to acquire it.
tuto.special-dice:
Red dice represent <0>special actions</0>.
This face allows you to <0>multiply the result of another die</0>.
You'll use it to multiply a die <2/>to obtain the missing population.
<1>At any time, click on a die to find out more.</1>
tuto.forest.pay: Now <0>discard all your remaining dice</0> to pay the cost of the <1>Forest</1>.
tuto.action1.pass: You have no more dice, click on <0>Pass</0> in the header to end your action phase.
tuto.war1.pass: After the action phase, it's possible to declare war on another player, but it's a bit early for that. Click on <0>Pass</0> in the header.
tuto.event-area.refill: At this stage, you draw cards until you again have 3 cards in your <0>Event Area</0>.
tuto.success: Before ending your turn, you can progress onto the <0>Accomplishments</0> board.
tuto.success.take:
Move your <0>Civilization Token</0> onto an Achievement token you can claim thanks to your <1>Forest</1>.
<1>Click on a token to find out its prerequisite</1>.
tuto.success.vp: Bravo! This token earns you 1 victory point, the <1>Forest<1/> as well, <0>so you have 2 victory points</0>.
tuto.turn1.over: Your turn is over! Click on <0>pass hand</0> in the header.
tuto.result-tokens: In each round, the active player plays a turn <0>with the dice</0>, then each other player also plays a turn with <0>the Result tokens</0> corresponding to the dice discarded by the active player.
tuto.round1.player2.1: NostroBoss returned the <1>Tiger</1> using a Result token...
tuto.round1.player2.2: NostroBoss acquired <1>Hunt</1>, and took an Achievement token.
tuto.round1.player3.1: Now it's Cécile's turn to play with the Result tokens.
tuto.round1.player3.2: Cécile acquired <1>Fishing</1>, and took an Accomplishment token.
tuto.round1.over: It's the end of the first round! The player to your left, NostroBoss, becomes the active player for the new round that begins. <0>It's his turn to play again.</0>
tuto.round2.player2: Now it's Cécile's turn, with the tokens corresponding to the NostroBoss dice.
tuto.round2.player2.over: Cécile picked up <1>Rites Funéraires</1> from Nostroboss and acquired it.
tuto.round2.you: Now it's your turn. You've received the tokens corresponding to the NostroBoss dice.
tuto.trade2: Turn over your Result <2/> token to exchange the <1>Tiger</1> with Cecile's <1>Wildcrafting</1> card.
tuto.wildcrafting: Move the <1>Wildcrafting</1> card into your civilization area to acquire it.
tuto.bonus.forest: The <1>Forest</1> card gives a <2/> bonus. Tilt the card to use its bonus to pay for the <1>Wildcrafting</1> card.
tuto.wildcrafting.pay: Finish paying for the <1>Wildcrafting</1> card by turning over your <2/> Result token.
tuto.bonus.wildcrafting: The <1>Wildcrafting</1> card gives a bonus <2/>, thanks to which you'll be able to acquire the <1>Australopithecus</1> card! <0>Move it to your Civilization Area and pay its cost.</0>
tuto.round2.actions.over: You have no more Result tokens, <0>Pass</0> to end your action phase.
tuto.universal:
This token is a <0>Universal Resource</0> token.
It can be used to declare war on another player, but also to pay <2/>, <3/>, <4/> or <5/>.
tuto.war2.pass: Let's save it for later. <0>Pass</0> to avoid declaring war this round.
tuto.success2: Now move on to the Achievement board and take a token.
tuto.decay.1: When you take an Accomplishment token, you must then put a card <0>into decay<0/>.
tuto.decay.2: Move the <1>Australopithecus</1> card under one of your other cards to put it in decay.
tuto.decay.3: A card in decay becomes <1>inactive</1>: it loses its name and bonuses, however <0>it still brings its symbol and victory points.</0>
tuto.decay.4: <0>Beware: each Resource or Population Bonus on a decaying card causes you to lose 2 victory points when counting down!</0>
tuto.decay.5:
<0>The decay rule doesn't apply if you only have one active card.</0>
For this reason, on your first turn you were able to take a token without putting a card into decay, as you only had the <1>Forest</1> card.
tuto.round2.pass: Now, <0>pass the hand</0>. Round 2 is over, so Cécile will roll the dice to start round 3.
tuto.war.1: Cécile declared war on you with her red die <2/>! She must not have liked your <1>Tiger</1>...
tuto.war.2: By waging war, she'll try to steal an Accomplishment token from you...
tuto.war.3:
To start with, she can tilt cards with a <0>Population bonus</0> in her Civilization area.
Each bonus tilted gives +1 to her army's strength!
tuto.war.4: She then rolls the 3 Population dice, which she adds to her army's strength.
tuto.war.5:
His army has a strength of 6!
You can also tilt Population bonuses before rolling the dice. Unfortunately, your <0>Gathering</0> card is already tilted and won't be of any use to you...
tuto.war.7: You have obtained 5 population points with the dice. Cécile's army beats you by one point! <0>She therefore steals an Accomplishment token with a maximum value of 1</0> (the difference between your two armies).
tuto.war.8: <0>The defender never steals tokens.</0>The attacker therefore takes no risk in declaring war, other than to annoy his opponent....
tuto.turn3: It's your turn again. Cécile has left a particularly poor dice combination... But maybe it's deliberate!
tuto.calamity:
A <0>calamity</0> has arrived in your Event area!
Don't panic... its negative effect (losing a card for <1>Wolves</1>) only applies if it's still in your Event area <0>at the end of a turn when you're the active player</0> (playing the dice).
You've got time to acquire it... or trade it in by then!
tuto.universal.gain1: If you do not acquire any new cards during a turn, you will earn a <0>Universal Resource</0> token at the end of the turn.
tuto.universal.gain2: You also earn a <0>Universal Resource</0> token by building a <2/>wonder, for example the <1>Megaliths</1> in Cecile's Events area.
tuto.age-end:
The game ends when a player takes <0>the rightmost</0> Accomplishment token from the board. Or, if the deck is empty.
In either case, <0>players complete the current round</0>.
tuto.good-luck:
Now it's up to you to beat NostroBoss and Cécile!
Don't forget: <0>at any time, you can click on an item to get a reminder of the associated rules.</0>
Good luck!
